title: Kane Bradley

(born 3 February 2000) is an Australian professional [rugby league](/wiki/Rugby_league "Rugby league") footballer who last played as a [second-row](/wiki/Rugby_league_positions#Second-row_forward "Rugby league positions"), [centre](/wiki/Rugby_league_positions#Centre "Rugby league positions") for the [Melbourne Storm](/wiki/Melbourne_Storm "Melbourne Storm") in the [National Rugby League](/wiki/National_Rugby_League "National Rugby League") (NRL).

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Australian rugby league player

Kane Bradley

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| Personal information | |
| Born | (2000-02-03) 3 February 2000 (age 26)  Sydney, New South Wales, Australia |
| Height | 196 cm (6 ft 5 in) |
| Weight | 107 kg (16 st 12 lb) |
| Playing information | |
| Position | [Wing](/wiki/Rugby_league_positions#Wing "Rugby league positions"), [Centre](/wiki/Rugby_league_positions#Centre "Rugby league positions"), [Second-row](/wiki/Rugby_league_positions#Second-row_forward "Rugby league positions") |
| | Club | | | | | | | | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | | Years | Team | Pld | T | G | FG | P | | 2021–22 | [North Qld Cowboys](/wiki/North_Queensland_Cowboys "North Queensland Cowboys") | 2 | 1 | 0 | 0 | 4 | | 2023–25 | [Melbourne Storm](/wiki/Melbourne_Storm "Melbourne Storm") | 11 | 5 | 0 | 0 | 20 | | 2026– | [Brisbane Broncos](/wiki/Brisbane_Broncos "Brisbane Broncos") | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 | |  | Total | 13 | 6 | 0 | 0 | 24 | | |
| Source: [[1]](#cite_note-1)  As of 29 August 2025 | |

**Kane Bradley** (born 3 February 2000) is an Australian professional [rugby league](/wiki/Rugby_league "Rugby league") footballer who last played as a [second-row](/wiki/Rugby_league_positions#Second-row_forward "Rugby league positions"), [centre](/wiki/Rugby_league_positions#Centre "Rugby league positions") for the [Melbourne Storm](/wiki/Melbourne_Storm "Melbourne Storm") in the [National Rugby League](/wiki/National_Rugby_League "National Rugby League") (NRL).

He previously played for the [North Queensland Cowboys](/wiki/North_Queensland_Cowboys "North Queensland Cowboys") in the [National Rugby League](/wiki/National_Rugby_League "National Rugby League") (NRL).

## Background

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Kane_Bradley&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Background")]

Bradley was born in Sydney, Australia.[[2]](#cite_note-Storm228-2)

He played his junior rugby league for Revesby Rhinos, [Penshurst RSL](/wiki/Penshurst_RSL "Penshurst RSL"), Chester Hill Hornets and Bankstown Sports[[2]](#cite_note-Storm228-2) and attended [Bass High School](/wiki/Bass_High_School "Bass High School").[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4)

## Playing career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Kane_Bradley&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Playing career")]

### Early career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Kane_Bradley&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Early career")]

In 2017 Bradley played for the [Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles](/wiki/Manly-Warringah_Sea_Eagles "Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles") in the [SG Ball Cup](/wiki/SG_Ball_Cup "SG Ball Cup").[[5]](#cite_note-5)[[6]](#cite_note-6)[[7]](#cite_note-7)

In 2018 he played for [St. George](/wiki/St._George_Dragons "St. George Dragons") in the SG Ball and was named in the New South Wales under-18 squad but did not play in their game against Queensland.[[8]](#cite_note-8)[[9]](#cite_note-9)

On 9 October 2018 Bradley signed with the [Wests Tigers](/wiki/Wests_Tigers "Wests Tigers"), joining their [NRL](/wiki/NRL "NRL") squad on a two-year development contract.[[10]](#cite_note-10)[[11]](#cite_note-11)

In 2019 he played for the Tigers' [Jersey Flegg Cup](/wiki/Jersey_Flegg_Cup "Jersey Flegg Cup") side and their [NSW Cup](/wiki/NSW_Cup "NSW Cup") affiliate, the [Western Suburbs Magpies](/wiki/Western_Suburbs_Magpies "Western Suburbs Magpies").[[12]](#cite_note-12)[[13]](#cite_note-13)

Bradley originally played as a [second-rower](/wiki/Rugby_league_positions#Second-row_forward "Rugby league positions"), but moved to the flanks where his pace and aerial threat could be better used.[[14]](#cite_note-14)[[15]](#cite_note-15)

### 2021

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Kane_Bradley&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: 2021")]

On 5 January Bradley joined the [North Queensland Cowboys](/wiki/North_Queensland_Cowboys "North Queensland Cowboys") on a two-year deal in a player swap deal with [Tukimihia Simpkins](/wiki/Tukimihia_Simpkins "Tukimihia Simpkins").[[16]](#cite_note-16)[[17]](#cite_note-17) He began the season playing for the [Mackay Cutters](/wiki/Mackay_Cutters "Mackay Cutters") in the [Queensland Cup](/wiki/Queensland_Cup "Queensland Cup").[[18]](#cite_note-18)

In Round 19 of the [2021 NRL season](/wiki/2021_NRL_season "2021 NRL season") Bradley made his NRL debut for [North Queensland](/wiki/North_Queensland_Cowboys "North Queensland Cowboys") against the [Melbourne Storm](/wiki/Melbourne_Storm "Melbourne Storm"), scoring a try in the 20–16 defeat.[[19]](#cite_note-19)[[20]](#cite_note-20)

On 2 August it was announced that Bradley would miss the rest of the [2021 NRL season](/wiki/2021_NRL_season "2021 NRL season") with a hand injury.[[21]](#cite_note-21)

### 2022

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Kane_Bradley&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: 2022")]

Bradley spent the entire 2022 season playing for the Mackay Cutters and was released by the North Queensland club at the end of the season.[[22]](#cite_note-22)[[23]](#cite_note-23)[[24]](#cite_note-24)

On 22 November he signed with the [Brisbane Tigers](/wiki/Brisbane_Tigers "Brisbane Tigers") in the [Queensland Cup](/wiki/Queensland_Cup "Queensland Cup") and underwent pre-season training with the [Melbourne Storm](/wiki/Melbourne_Storm "Melbourne Storm"), their NRL affiliate.[[25]](#cite_note-25)

### 2023

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Kane_Bradley&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: 2023")]

In round 2 of the [2023 NRL season](/wiki/2023_NRL_season "2023 NRL season"), Bradley made his [Melbourne Storm](/wiki/Melbourne_Storm "Melbourne Storm") debut against the [Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs](/wiki/Canterbury-Bankstown_Bulldogs "Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs"). He was presented with his debut jersey (cap 228).[[2]](#cite_note-Storm228-2) He also went onto win the 2023 Queensland Cup with the [Brisbane Tigers](/wiki/Brisbane_Tigers "Brisbane Tigers") where he started at centre and scored a try in the grand final.[[26]](#cite_note-26)

### 2026

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Kane_Bradley&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: 2026")]

In Mid-December 2025, the Broncos confirmed that they had signed Bradley on a train and trial contract.[[27]](#cite_note-27)
